---
title: "Org-mode Fundamentals"
title-block-style: none
subtitle: "Master Emacs' powerful organization and note-taking system in just 2 hours"
description: "Learn the basics of Org-mode, a powerful plain text system for notes, planning, and project management within Emacs, featuring outlines, todo lists, scheduling, and much more."
page-layout: custom
toc: false
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .m { --c: #F5A623; }
        .ml-content { max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 1.5rem; }
        .ml-content h1, .ml-content h2 { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.02em; margin-top: 3rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; padding-bottom: 0.75rem; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06); }
        [data-bs-theme="dark"] .ml-content h1, [data-bs-theme="dark"] .ml-content h2 { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); }
        .ml-content h3 { font-size: 1.15rem; font-weight: 600; margin-top: 2rem; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }
        .ml-content h1:first-child, .ml-content h2:first-child { margin-top: 0; }
        .ml-content p { color: var(--g, #86868b); line-height: 1.7; margin-bottom: 1rem; }
        .ml-content ul, .ml-content ol { color: var(--g, #86868b); line-height: 1.8; padding-left: 1.25rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; }
        .ml-content li { padding: 0.2rem 0; }
        .ml-content strong { color: inherit; font-weight: 600; }
        .ml-meta { display: flex; gap: 1.5rem; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 2rem; }
        .ml-meta-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-size: 0.9rem; color: var(--g, #86868b); }
        .ml-meta-item i { color: var(--c); }
        .ml-card { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.02); border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06); border-radius: 16px; padding: 2rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; }
        [data-bs-theme="dark"] .ml-card { background: rgba(245,166,35,0.04); border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); }
        .ml-card h3, .ml-card h4 { margin-top: 0; border: none; padding-bottom: 0; }
        .ml-related { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 1.25rem; }
        @media (max-width: 767px) { .ml-related { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } }
        </style>
date-modified: last-modified
categories:
  - productivity
  - organization
  - note-taking
  - emacs
---

```{=html}
<div class="m">

<div class="ev-hero" style="min-height:50vh;">
  <div class="ev-hero-bg" style="background-image:url('/assets/images/stock/hacker-screens.webp');"></div>
  <div class="ev-hero-content">
    <div class="ev-hero-tag" style="background:#F5A623;">Microlearning</div>
    <h1>Org-mode Fundamentals</h1>
    <p>Learn the basics of this powerful organization and note-taking system for Emacs in just 2 hours</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {.ml-content}

# Session Overview

This 2-hour microlearning session introduces Org-mode, a powerful plain text system for notes, todo lists, planning, and project management within the Emacs text editor. Org-mode combines the simplicity of plain text with the power of a complete organizational system, allowing you to manage tasks, take notes, create documents, and much more. In this session, you'll learn the fundamental concepts and commands to start using Org-mode for personal productivity and information management.

## What You'll Learn

- Set up and configure Org-mode in Emacs
- Create structured documents with headings and outlines
- Manage tasks with TODO states and priorities
- Schedule tasks and set deadlines
- Take effective notes with Org-mode
- Export Org documents to various formats (HTML, PDF, etc.)
- Integrate Org-mode into your daily workflow

# Session Curriculum

## Part 1: Introduction to Org-mode (30 minutes)

- What is Org-mode and why use it?
- Setting up Org-mode in Emacs
- Basic Org file structure and syntax
- Creating and navigating outlines with headings
- Text formatting and markup options
- Working with lists (ordered, unordered, description)
- Links and cross-references

## Part 2: Task Management and Planning (40 minutes)

- Creating TODO items
- Customizing TODO states (DONE, WAITING, CANCELLED, etc.)
- Setting priorities
- Tagging and categorizing items
- Scheduling tasks and setting deadlines
- Creating recurring tasks
- Using the agenda view to see scheduled items
- Tracking time with the clock feature

## Part 3: Advanced Features and Workflows (40 minutes)

- Note-taking strategies with Org-mode
- Working with tables
- Code blocks and literate programming
- Capturing ideas and information with org-capture
- Archiving completed tasks
- Exporting Org files to other formats (HTML, PDF, etc.)
- Using templates for consistent structures
- Basic customization options

## Part 4: Integration and Best Practices (10 minutes)

- Setting up a sustainable Org system
- Common Org-mode workflows
- Mobile options for Org files
- Resources for continued learning
- Tips and tricks from experienced users

# Learning Outcomes

By the end of this microlearning session, you will be able to:

- Create structured Org documents with headings, lists, and formatting
- Set up a task management system with TODO states and scheduling
- Use the agenda view to see upcoming tasks and deadlines
- Capture notes and ideas efficiently with org-capture
- Export your Org documents to different formats
- Implement best practices for organizing your information
- Know where to find help and resources for continued learning

# Teaching Methods

This session combines:

- Live demonstrations
- Hands-on exercises
- Template-based learning
- Real-world use cases
- Interactive challenges

# Target Audience

This microlearning session is ideal for:

- Emacs users looking to enhance their productivity
- Knowledge workers who manage multiple projects
- Writers, researchers, and academics
- Software developers and technical professionals
- Anyone interested in plain-text productivity systems

# Prerequisites

- Basic familiarity with Emacs (completion of our "Emacs Essentials" microlearning session is recommended but not required)
- Bring your laptop with Emacs and Org-mode installed (installation instructions will be provided)

# Materials

All participants will receive:

- Org-mode cheat sheet with key commands and syntax
- Template files for different use cases (notes, projects, journals)
- Sample Emacs configuration for Org-mode
- Recommended Org-mode packages and extensions
- List of resources for continued learning

:::

```{=html}
<div class="m-s">
  <div class="ml-content">
    <div class="ml-card">
      <h3>Your Instructor</h3>
      <p><strong>CHENIST Team</strong> — Productivity Researcher & Emacs Expert</p>
      <p>Dr. Liu has been using Org-mode for over a decade to manage her research, teaching, and personal projects. She has published several articles on productivity systems and regularly speaks at Emacs conferences.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="ml-card">
      <h3>What Participants Say</h3>
      <p><em>"I've tried dozens of productivity apps, but nothing compares to the flexibility of Org-mode. This session gave me a solid foundation to build my own system that actually fits my workflow."</em></p>
      <p><strong>— Nathan T., Project Manager</strong></p>
    </div>

    <h3>Related Offerings</h3>
    <div class="ml-related">
      <div class="ml-card">
        <h4>Emacs Essentials</h4>
        <p>Learn the fundamentals of the Emacs text editor, the foundation for using Org-mode effectively.</p>
        <a href="/academy/microlearning/emacs/" class="m-bo">Learn More</a>
      </div>
      <div class="ml-card">
        <h4>Personal Knowledge Management</h4>
        <p>A comprehensive course on building systems for managing information and knowledge across different tools and contexts.</p>
        <a href="/academy/courses.html" class="m-bo">Learn More</a>
      </div>
      <div class="ml-card">
        <h4>Advanced Org-mode Workshop</h4>
        <p>A full-day deep dive into advanced Org-mode features, including complex project management, publishing, and customization.</p>
        <a href="/academy/trainings.html" class="m-bo">Learn More</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="m-cta">
  <div class="container">
    <h2>Org-mode Fundamentals</h2>
    <p>Interested in this program? Get in touch to learn more.</p>
    <a href="/contact.html" class="m-lk">Contact Us</a>
  </div>
</div>

</div>
```
